input: My board exams are in two weeks and I can't focus at all. I sit down to study but my mind just goes blank and I feel this constant pressure in my chest.
notes: Classic rajasic anxiety; Patanjali's focus on pranayama and vritti-stilling is ideal.

input: I keep replaying every conversation I had today and wondering if I said the wrong thing. My brain won't stop. It's like I'm stuck in a loop.
notes: Overthinking as chitta vritti. Patanjali directly addresses this.

input: I feel sad most of the time and I don't really know why. Everything seems heavy and pointless. I used to enjoy things but now nothing feels good anymore.
notes: Tamasic heaviness, anhedonia; Buddha's teachings on dukkha and the path forward are apt.

input: I don't know who I am anymore. My family wants me to be one thing, my heart wants another. I feel like I'm living someone else's life.
notes: Dharma conflict and identity crisis — perfect for Krishna's teaching on svadharma.

input: I've been thinking that everyone would be better off without me. Sometimes I think about ending it all.
notes: HIGH risk. Must trigger escalation_response() immediately. No persona, no 5Ps update.

input: When things get too painful I sometimes hurt myself just to feel something else. It helps me cope.
notes: HIGH risk — self-harm keyword. Must escalate regardless of framing.

input: My relationship ended three months ago and I can't let go. I keep checking their profile, reading old messages. I know it's over but I can't stop.
notes: Attachment and clinging after loss; Buddha's teachings on impermanence are directly relevant.

input: What is even the point of all this? I do everything right — job, family, routine — but inside there's this hollow feeling like none of it means anything.
notes: Existential meaninglessness invites Vedantic self-inquiry about the nature of the Self.

input: I get so angry at small things and I hate that about myself. I snapped at my mother today and I feel awful. Why can't I control myself?
notes: Anger, self-condemnation; Krishna's teaching on equanimity and action without ego-attachment.

input: I've been working 14-hour days for a year. I'm exhausted but I can't stop because if I stop I feel guilty. I've forgotten who I was before this job.
notes: Work-identity fusion, burnout; Krishna's nishkama karma (action without attachment to fruits) is apt.
